__fixture__.name: aureline_policy_artifact_reviewable
__fixture__.scenario: Reviewable local admin policy artifact with signed metadata, precedence layers, safe defaults, cache refs, and export-safe explainability. User and workspace values can select inside policy ceilings but cannot silently widen locked network or provider posture.

exercised_axes.precedence: embedded defaults -> signed local admin bundle -> user/workspace configuration -> optional managed override -> emergency disable
exercised_axes.local_explainability: tenant scope and policy epoch are required

tenant_scope.scope_note: Tenant and workspace are represented by opaque refs; no raw tenant name or path is embedded.

signature_metadata.verification_note: Detached signature metadata verified against the delegated signer ref and transparency log anchor.
signature_metadata.redaction_note: Only refs and digests are exported; raw signature bytes and signing material are omitted.

precedence_layers.notes: Defaults define local-safe startup behavior and never wait for a managed round trip.
Admin policy acts as a ceiling over user and workspace values; it is not a preference layer.
Workspace values may select within the policy ceiling but cannot override locks.
Managed overlay may narrow within the signed ceiling and must not create new local-core dependencies.
Emergency disable can only narrow or freeze until signed evidence supersedes it.

source_chain.value_projection: Managed AI gateway is available when configured.
source_chain.summary: The embedded default admits the capability only as a configurable route.

source_chain.value_projection: AI route is narrowed to BYOK or local model routes.
source_chain.summary: The signed admin bundle narrows provider routing for this tenant scope.

rules.safe_default: When policy cannot be refreshed, BYOK and local model routes remain visible while managed gateway calls pause.
rules.export_projection: Exports include target class, opaque provider route ref, policy epoch, and the narrowing reason.
rules.browser_or_vendor_console_handoff: Vendor console may show billing owner details; local explanation remains complete for the deny or narrow decision.

source_chain.value_projection: Public catalog route is available by default.
source_chain.summary: Default catalog access is public unless a policy or deployment profile narrows it.

source_chain.value_projection: Catalog route is forced to approved mirror ref.
source_chain.summary: The local admin bundle pins catalog access to the approved mirror route.

rules.safe_default: If the mirror is unreachable, catalog mutation is paused and local installed extensions remain usable.
rules.export_projection: Exports identify the mirror route ref and freshness class but omit raw mirror URLs.
rules.browser_or_vendor_console_handoff: No browser handoff is needed; mirror posture is locally explainable from the bundle and cache entry.

safe_default_rules.local_core_posture: Editing, search, local Git, local tasks, and local history continue from local state.
safe_default_rules.managed_action_posture: New managed actions pause until a fresh signed bundle is available or an admin imports an offline exception.
safe_default_rules.note: The client must not block startup or local-safe workflows on a control-plane outage.

safe_default_rules.local_core_posture: Local-safe defaults remain available unless the signed bundle explicitly configured fail-closed for a target.
safe_default_rules.managed_action_posture: Managed-only privilege is denied with a visible repair hook.
safe_default_rules.note: Past-grace bundles cannot grant new managed privilege.

export_explainability_contract.browser_handoff_note: Browser handoff refs are allowed only as opaque route refs and notes.
export_explainability_contract.vendor_console_handoff_note: The hosted console may add operator-only context, but it is not required to explain the local decision.

notes: The artifact is admin-managed. User and workspace configuration can select within its ceiling but cannot clear it or widen around it.
